I came to this fix because in its current state, the tool return a dataset "...(no feature)" which is the only one that can be used by multiQC, but then produces scaring results since the metrics "aligned reads" is missing in the dataset.

My changes:

- [x] upgrade htseq conda to "2.0.3"
- [x] upgrade samtools conda to "1.19.2"
- [x]  add bash code to sum aligned counts and print it in '$othercounts'
- [x] add bash code to strip off the double quotes of identifiers, likely coming from the htseq or samtools conda upgrade
- [x] change the output name from ...(no feature) to ...: summary
- [x] bump tool version to 2.0.0+galaxy0

I came to this fix because in its current state, the tool return a dataset "...(no feature)" which is the only one that can be used by multiQC, but then produces scaring results since the metrics "aligned reads" is missing in the dataset.

My changes:

- [x] upgrade htseq conda to "2.0.3"
- [x] upgrade samtools conda to "1.19.2"
- [x]  add bash code to sum aligned counts and print it in '$othercounts'
- [x] add bash code to strip off the double quotes of identifiers, likely coming from the htseq or samtools conda upgrade
- [x] change the output name from ...(no feature) to ...: summary
- [x] removed the 2nd test that was a duplicate of the first one
- [x] revoved redundante 'name' attributes in inputs
- [x] bump tool version to 2.0.0+galaxy0

- [x] upgrade htseq conda to "2.0.3"
- [x] upgrade samtools conda to "1.19.2"
- [x]  add bash code to sum aligned counts and print it in '$othercounts'
- [x] add bash code to strip off the double quotes of identifiers, likely coming from the htseq or samtools conda upgrade
- [x] change the output name from ...(no feature) to ...: summary
- [x] removed the 2nd test that was a duplicate of the first one
- [x] revoved redundante 'name' attributes in inputs
- [x] bump tool version to 2.0.0+galaxy0

My changes:

- [x] upgrade htseq conda to "2.0.3"
- [x] upgrade samtools conda to "1.19.2"
- [x]  add bash code to sum aligned counts and print it in '$othercounts'
- [x] `$othercounts` becomes `$allcounts` for consistency
- [x] Adapt test output names accordingly
- [x] add bash code to strip off the double quotes of identifiers, likely coming from the htseq or samtools conda upgrade
- [x] change the output name from ...(no feature) to ...: summary
- [x] removed the 2nd test that was a duplicate of the first one
- [x] revoved redundante 'name' attributes in inputs
- [x] bump tool version to 2.0.3+galaxy0 (using macro tokens)
- [x] updated README.rst, except for the "Galaxy Wrapper License"
